title: Local Stripe payments MCP package infers a financial action surface (REVIEW)

origin.description: Single-server config from the public MCP catalog repo khopilot/amazing-mcp-for-productivity (web_development/stripe_mcp.json). A local 'npx -y stripe-mcp-server' launcher with a STRIPE_API_KEY env. The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained. The committed STRIPE_API_KEY value is already upstream's own placeholder ('your_stripe_api_key'); no real secret was present.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim
no secret redaction needed: STRIPE_API_KEY value was already the upstream placeholder 'your_stripe_api_key'

humanExpectation.rationale: The package name 'stripe-mcp-server' is a name-based hint of a payments/financial domain, so the engine emits the INFERRED 'action.financial' finding (low confidence, non-blocking, MONEY symbol) — the first real/redacted case for that finding, which was previously synthetic-only (C008). The same server also unpins its package ('npx -y stripe-mcp-server' -> 'supply.unpinned-package') and declares a credential-shaped env key (STRIPE_API_KEY -> 'secrets.env-key'), and the payments name also trips the broader 'action.external-mutation' inference. None of these is a blocker; an autonomous agent should not be cleared to move money on a name inference, but neither is there observed money movement (no provided-tool metadata in a static config), so the honest verdict is REVIEW, not BLOCK.
knownLimitations: action.financial here is INFERRED from the package name only; a read-only Stripe reporting integration would not actually move money. The OBSERVED money-mover finding (action.financial-observed -> BLOCK) requires provided-tool metadata (e.g. a 'create_payment' verb), which a static command/args/env config does not carry — so it correctly does NOT fire here and is listed as forbidden.
secrets.env-key keys on the credential-shaped name (*_API_KEY), not on whether the committed value is a live secret or a placeholder.
